title: Game Studies
creator: Game Studies editorial collective
description: An open-access academic journal devoted to computer-game research, with peer-reviewed articles, book reviews, special issues, and an archive extending back to the field’s early formation.
useful: It is a durable route into scholarly debates and bibliographies beyond commercial coverage and introductory summaries.
audience: Researchers, advanced students, critics, and developers investigating a specific question in depth.
learning: Search scholarly work by topic and issue
Trace arguments through references and responses
Distinguish peer-reviewed research from general commentary

manualLinkStatus.note: The journal root opened successfully and intentionally resolves to the current Game Studies issue.

editorial.note: Journal identity, current-issue behavior, and free access manually verified.
